source_documents: https://www.sebi.gov.in (SEBI Prohibition of Insider Trading Regulations 2015 + enforcement orders)
category: Financial statement fraud - overstated revenue/assets, understated liabilities
risk_signals: Reported revenue and profit growth not matched by operating cash flow
Fictitious receivables, cash balances, or sham related-party transactions inflating the books
Auditor concerns, frequent restatements, or aggressive revenue-recognition changes
analyst_reasoning: Financial-statement fraud makes a company look healthier than it is, usually to support the share price or secure funding. As ACFE data notes it is the least frequent but most costly fraud type; the enduring tell (as in the Satyam case) is a divergence between reported profits and real cash, propped up by fictitious assets.
investigation_steps: Reconcile reported earnings against operating cash flow and independent bank confirmations
Scrutinise related-party transactions and revenue-recognition timing for manipulation
Assess auditor changes, restatements, and internal-control weaknesses
decision: Escalate for forensic audit, restrict reliance on the statements, and refer to the securities regulator where investors are affected.
outcome: Statement fraud collapses spectacularly when the cash-vs-profit gap is exposed, as in Satyam; cash-based verification is the key test.
lessons_learned: Profit that is not backed by cash is the enduring financial-statement-fraud signal
Fictitious assets and sham related-party deals are the classic mechanisms
Auditor turnover and restatements are meaningful early warnings
